title: Oligochaetochilus pusillus

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Oligochaetochilus pusillus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oligochaetochilus pusillus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and fever.
